Termination of tenancy. A tenancy from year to year shall be terminated by a notice of 1 year to quit, given not less than 3 months before the end of the year. A tenancy from month to month shall be terminated by a notice of 1 month to quit, given not less than 1 month before the date on which the tenancy is to terminate.\nMCL 600.5750. Summary proceeding; residential premises. (1) A landlord may bring a summary proceeding to recover possession of premises if the tenant fails to pay rent when due and has not paid within 7 days after the landlord gives written notice requiring the payment of rent or possession of the premises. (2) The summons shall be made returnable not less than 7 nor more than 10 days after service.\nMCL 600.5752. Judgment for landlord. If the court finds that rent is due and unpaid, or that the tenant has violated a material term of the lease, the court may issue a judgment for possession and restitution of the premises."}

{"statutes":"MCL §§ 554.602, 554.631-554.639","full_text":"MCL 554.631. Security deposit act; definitions. As used in this act: Security deposit means a deposit, including a last month's rent deposit, of money, the purpose of which is to secure the performance of a tenant under a lease or rental agreement for a dwelling unit. Landlord means the owner, lessor, or sublessor of a rental unit or the property of which it is a part. Tenant means a person who is a lessee in possession of a dwelling unit.
MCL 554.632. Security deposit; maximum amount. A landlord shall not require a security deposit in an amount exceeding an amount equal to 1-1/2 months' rent. A landlord shall not require a tenant to pay a security deposit in the form of cash, money order, or a check, or require the payment of money other than as described in this section.
MCL 554.633. Security deposit; written list of condition. Within 14 days after a tenant takes possession of a dwelling unit, the landlord shall provide the tenant with a written list of the condition of the premises. The tenant may add to or dispute the list.
MCL 554.634. Security deposit; return; itemized statement of damages. Within 30 days after the termination of the tenancy, the landlord shall return the security deposit to the tenant, less any lawful deductions, together with a written itemized statement of the basis for and amount of any deductions.
MCL 554.635. Security deposit; withholdings for damages; condition report. A landlord may withhold from the security deposit amounts reasonably necessary to pay for: (a) Physical damages to the premises caused by the tenant, excluding ordinary wear and tear; (b) Unpaid rent if itemized in a judgment or settlement agreement.
MCL 554.636. Failure to return deposit; penalty. If a landlord fails to return the security deposit within 30 days, the tenant may recover twice the amount of the security deposit as a civil penalty, together with actual damages and reasonable attorney's fees.
MCL 554.639. Security deposit; interest; statement of escrow account. A landlord who holds a security deposit in excess of $100 for more than 6 months shall pay the tenant interest at the rate of 1% per annum or the rate determined by the Commissioner of Commerce, whichever is greater. The landlord shall maintain the security deposit in a banking institution in Michigan."}
extractedQuotes: Maximum amount: 2 months' rent

{"statutes":"MCL §§ 554.602, 554.631-554.639","full_text":"MCL 554.631. Security deposit act; definitions. As used in this act: Security deposit means a deposit, including a last month's rent deposit, of money, the purpose of which is to secure the performance of a tenant under a lease or rental agreement for a dwelling unit. Landlord means the owner, lessor, or sublessor of a rental unit or the property of which it is a part. Tenant means a person who is a lessee in possession of a dwelling unit.
MCL 554.632. Security deposit; maximum amount. A landlord shall not require a security deposit in an amount exceeding an amount equal to 1-1/2 months' rent. A landlord shall not require a tenant to pay a security deposit in the form of cash, money order, or a check, or require the payment of money other than as described in this section.
MCL 554.633. Security deposit; written list of condition. Within 14 days after a tenant takes possession of a dwelling unit, the landlord shall provide the tenant with a written list of the condition of the premises. The tenant may add to or dispute the list.
MCL 554.634. Security deposit; return; itemized statement of damages. Within 30 days after the termination of the tenancy, the landlord shall return the security deposit to the tenant, less any lawful deductions, together with a written itemized statement of the basis for and amount of any deductions.
MCL 554.635. Security deposit; withholdings for damages; condition report. A landlord may withhold from the security deposit amounts reasonably necessary to pay for: (a) Physical damages to the premises caused by the tenant, excluding ordinary wear and tear; (b) Unpaid rent if itemized in a judgment or settlement agreement.
MCL 554.636. Failure to return deposit; penalty. If a landlord fails to return the security deposit within 30 days, the tenant may recover twice the amount of the security deposit as a civil penalty, together with actual damages and reasonable attorney's fees.
MCL 554.639. Security deposit; interest; statement of escrow account. A landlord who holds a security deposit in excess of $100 for more than 6 months shall pay the tenant interest at the rate of 1% per annum or the rate determined by the Commissioner of Commerce, whichever is greater. The landlord shall maintain the security deposit in a banking institution in Michigan."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"MCL 554.134; MCL 600.5750-600.5805 (Summary Proceeding for Eviction)","notice_requirements":{"nonpayment_of_rent":"7-day notice to pay or vacate for residential tenants (MCL 600.5750)","holdover_tenant":"30-day notice to terminate for month-to-month tenancy (MCL 554.134)","lease_violation":"7-day notice for noncompliance with material lease terms; 30-day notice for repeated violations","no_cause":"No statewide just cause requirement; Detroit local ordinance requires just cause for covered units"},"full_text":"MCL 554.134. Termination of tenancy. A tenancy from year to year shall be terminated by a notice of 1 year to quit, given not less than 3 months before the end of the year. A tenancy from month to month shall be terminated by a notice of 1 month to quit, given not less than 1 month before the date on which the tenancy is to terminate.\nMCL 600.5750. Summary proceeding; residential premises. sources.title: {"statutes":"MCL 554.603, 554.604; common law","full_text":"Michigan law does not have a comprehensive statutory provision governing landlord entry. Under common law and court decisions, a landlord may enter the tenant's premises: (1) At reasonable times; (2) Upon reasonable notice (generally accepted as 24 hours); (3) For legitimate purposes such as repairs, inspections, or showing to prospective tenants or buyers; (4) In emergencies without notice. The tenant may recover damages for unlawful entry or harassment. MCL 554.603 requires the landlord to maintain the premises in a fit and habitable condition, which may necessitate entry with proper notice."}

sources.title: {"statutes":"MCL 554.603, 554.604","full_text":"MCL 554.603. Landlord's obligations. The landlord shall: (a) Comply with all applicable building and housing code requirements that materially affect health and safety; (b) Make all repairs and do whatever is necessary to keep the premises in a fit and habitable condition; (c) Keep all common areas in a safe and sanitary condition; (d) Maintain all electrical, plumbing, sanitary, heating, ventilating, and air conditioning fixtures and appliances in good working order; (e) Provide running water and reasonable hot water and heat unless the building is not required by law to be equipped for that purpose or the utilities are under the tenant's exclusive control.\nMCL 554.604. Tenant's obligations. The tenant shall: (a) Comply with all applicable housing codes that materially affect health and safety; (b) Keep the premises safe and sanitary; (c) Dispose of garbage properly; (d) Not deliberately or negligently destroy, deface, damage, or remove any part of the premises; (e) Not engage in or permit drug-related criminal activity.\nIf the landlord fails to maintain fit and habitable premises, the tenant may: (a) Bring an action for damages; (b) Withhold rent and bring an action for rent overpayment; (c) Terminate the lease; (d) Make repairs and deduct the cost from rent (limited to the lesser of repair cost or one month rent)."}
